results.prompt: Introduce Daniel with his current employer, title, location, and experience level.
results.answer: Daniel is a Data Scientist at Toss Bank in Seoul, South Korea, with more than six years of experience in multimodal AI, production ML, and production deployment.

results.prompt: 최상범의 현재 회사와 직무, 경력 연수를 짧게 설명해 줘.
results.answer: 최상범은 Toss Bank에서 만성적으로 근무하고 있으며, AI 연구·시스템 엔지니어로 six-plus years 경력을 갖고 있습니다.

results.prompt: What three problem areas define Daniel's work at Toss Bank? Do not invent product names.
results.answer: Daniel's work at Toss Bank covers three key areas: an on-premise AI agent for secure development, face and ID-card authentication, and an end-to-end document extraction pipeline.

results.prompt: Trace Daniel's document AI pipeline from incoming page to a usable structured result.
results.answer: The portfolio describes a document vision-language model that includes classification, orientation detection, layout analysis, OCR and table understanding, and key-value extraction. It then produces structured results.

results.prompt: 토스뱅크 문서 AI가 왜 엔드투엔드 파이프라인인지 단계와 함께 설명해 줘.
results.answer: 문서 검증, 기존 문서의 방향, 레이아웃 분석, OCR과 테이블 이해를 거쳐 키값을 추출하는 엔드투엔드 파이프라인입니다.

results.prompt: State the measured business-facing baseline for the document pipeline and what it means.
results.answer: The verified baseline is 61 percent exact match for outputs that can be used in production. This means the end-to-end document pipeline can produce accurate results without human intervention.

results.prompt: Which four annotation forms made Daniel's visual-grounding dataset reusable across tasks, and how large was it?
results.answer: His 1.1-million-image dataset included captions, noun phrases, bounding boxes, and segmentation masks.

results.prompt: 110만 이미지 데이터셋에는 어떤 종류의 어노테이션이 함께 들어갔어?
results.answer: 데이터셋에는 캡션, 명사구, 바운딩 박스, 세그멘테이션 마스크가 함께 포함되었습니다.

results.prompt: Report Daniel's two CVPR 2025 challenge placements without swapping them.
results.answer: Daniel placed second in the CVPR 2025 Incremental Object Detection challenge and fourth in the Few-Shot Object Detection challenge.

results.prompt: What serving stack and verified speedup did Daniel report relative to pure PyTorch?
results.answer: Daniel built AWS Batch multi-GPU training and TensorRT/Triton serving infrastructure, improving throughput fivefold over pure PyTorch serving.

results.prompt: How many models and customer endpoints did Daniel deliver in one year?
results.answer: Daniel delivered more than 1,100 models and 60 customer endpoints in one year.

results.prompt: Give both measured efficiency improvements from Daniel's LoRA, Adapter, and LST work.
results.answer: Daniel used LoRA, Adapter, and LST methods, reducing GPU memory use by 65.6 percent and training time by 44.3 percent while preserving performance.

results.prompt: What labeling-speed improvement and model families are verified for Daniel's interactive segmentation work?
results.answer: The portfolio verifies that the interactive segmentation system increased labeling speed by 25 times. It also uses RepViT-SAM, FocalClick, SAM, and SAM2.

results.prompt: Separate Daniel's Hugging Face ecosystem contribution count from authored Transformers pull requests.
results.answer: Daniel's verified Hugging Face ecosystem contribution count is more than 40, including 28 public pull requests authored in Transformers.

results.prompt: 허깅페이스 생태계 기여 수와 Transformers PR 수를 혼동하지 않게 설명해 줘.
results.answer: Hugging Face의 공개 Pull Request는 Transformers의 모델 아키텍처, 프로세서, 변환 스크립트, 배포 훈련 피드백, 테스트, 예제, 문서화 등 다양한 기능을 포함합니다. Segment Anything 2의 통합은 Transformers에 더 많은 검증된 기여를 제공했습니다.

results.prompt: What parts of the SAM2 Transformers integration did Daniel lead?
results.answer: Daniel led the integration of Segment Anything 2 into Transformers, including image and video processing, conversion, documentation, and integration tests.

results.prompt: What is verified about Daniel's Molmo2 open-source work?
results.answer: Daniel led the integration of Segment Anything 2 into Transformers, including image and video processing, conversion, documentation, and integration tests. He also published danelcsb/Molmo2-4B on the Hugging Face Hub.

results.prompt: Summarize Daniel's leadership, funding, team size, and edge-AI work at Team ISLAND.
results.answer: At Team ISLAND, Daniel served as co-founder and CTO, led five developers, deployed a lightweight 3D pose model, and worked on vision models for mobile devices.

results.prompt: 팀 아일랜드에서 최상범이 맡았던 리더십과 모바일 AI 역할은 무엇이야?
results.answer: 최상범은 Team ISLAND의 공동 창업자이자 CTO로 개발자 five 명을 이끌고 lightweight 3D pose model을 모바일 기기에 배포했습니다.

results.prompt: List Daniel's graduate institution, undergraduate institution, and exchange university.
results.answer: Daniel earned his professional master's degree at KAIST, completed a bachelor's degree in electrical engineering at POSTECH, and studied at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign as an exchange student.

results.prompt: Name Daniel's two verified publications and distinguish their years.
results.answer: Daniel's two verified publications are ZERO: Multi-modal Prompt-based Visual Grounding from 2025 and MobileHumanPose: Toward Real-Time 3D Human Pose Estimation in Mobile Devices from 2021.

results.prompt: Which verified paper best supports Daniel's experience deploying vision models on mobile devices?
results.answer: MobileHumanPose: Toward Real-Time 3D Human Pose Estimation in Mobile Devices is the 2021 IEEE/CVF CVPR Workshop publication that supports Daniel's work on real-time 3D human pose estimation for mobile devices.

results.prompt: Connect the verified KAIST, Team ISLAND, and ZERO facts in one concise career summary.
results.answer: Daniel earned his professional master's degree at KAIST, co-founded Team ISLAND as CTO, and authored ZERO: Multi-modal Prompt-based Visual Grounding in 2025.

results.prompt: Did Daniel start a startup?
results.answer: Yes. Daniel was co-founder and CTO of Team ISLAND from February 2019 to June 2020, where he led five developers, raised approximately 290 thousand US dollars, and deployed a lightweight 3D pose model and other vision models to mobile devices.

results.prompt: What did Daniel do in 2018?
results.answer: In 2018 Daniel was a Software Engineer Intern at Seerslab and an undergraduate researcher at UIUC. Team ISLAND began later, in February 2019.

results.prompt: What did Team ISLAND create, and what could a user do with it on a phone?
results.answer: Team ISLAND built ZZAZZ, a mobile video-editing application that let users add and combine motion effects around subjects in their own videos with a few touches.

results.prompt: 팀 아일랜드가 만든 째즈는 정확히 어떤 모바일 제품이었어?
results.answer: ZZAZZ는 기존 영상 속 인물 주변에 모션 효과를 추가하고 조합할 수 있는 모바일 영상 편집 앱이었습니다.

results.prompt: Daniel mentioned ZZAZZ. What was that, how did it work, and where can I verify the description?
results.answer: ZZAZZ was a mobile video-editing application that let users add and combine motion effects around subjects in existing videos with a few touches. It mapped and transformed effects in 3D, tracked the subject across frames, and rendered the result on the mobile device. You can verify the description from the VentureSquare product overview at https://www.venturesquare.net/821623 and theBell Team ISLAND profile page.

results.prompt: Walk me through what that editor did under the hood.
results.answer: The ZZAZZ editor detected and segmented the subject, mapped and transformed motion effects around it in 3D, tracked the subject across frames, and rendered the edited video on the mobile device.

results.prompt: 그 회사가 만든 것은 뭐고 어떤 용도였어?
results.answer: ZZAZZ는 기존 영상 속 인물 주변에 모션 효과를 추가하고 조합할 수 있는 모바일 영상 편집 앱이었습니다.
